page: Google Cloud Platform x4-960-12t-metal is a bare-metal server instance belonging to the x4 server family. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 960 dedicated vCPUs and a total memory capacity of 12,288.0 GB. This configuration yields a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12.8 GB per core, positioning it as a memory-optimized platform designed for massive in-memory workloads.

The instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration. The lack of hardware accelerators like GPUs limits its applicability for direct machine learning training workloads, while the absence of local storage requires architectural planning for disk-heavy operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal nature of the x4-960-12t-metal provides direct hardware access without virtualization overhead, maximizing resource density for large-scale enterprise applications. This server is designed for massive in-memory databases, large-scale SAP HANA deployments, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data warehousing workloads that require substantial memory capacity alongside high-core-count parallel processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform x4-960-12t-metal is a bare-metal server in the x4 family, featuring 960 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 12,288.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.8 GB per vCPU, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence and limiting its use in GPU-accelerated computing. By offering direct hardware access without virtualization overhead, this bare-metal server delivers high resource density and predictable performance. It is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, SAP HANA deployments, and enterprise data warehousing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform x4-960-12t-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 960 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 12,288 GB of RAM, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform x4-960-12t-metal is a bare-metal server with 960 dedicated vCPUs and 12 TB of RAM, optimized for in-memory databases.
tagline: A bare-metal platform featuring nine hundred sixty dedicated cores and twelve terabytes of memory for massive in-memory enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 960 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for massive parallel processing
12,288 GB of RAM providing 12.8 GB of memory per core
No local storage or integrated GPUs included
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and SAP HANA
